description: Core eval cho research agent: routing đúng tool + đúng args, hỏi lại khi thiếu (ask_user), xác nhận trước khi gửi, và xử lý câu ngoài phạm vi / không cần tool. Chấm trên tool_calls + args (subset). KHÔNG sửa file này — chỉ thêm case vào eval_group.json.

items.title: So you’ve heard these AI terms and nodded along; let’s fix that - TechCrunch
items.summary: Artificial intelligence is changing the world, and simultaneously inventing a whole new language to describe how it’s doing it. But it generally refers to AI that’s more capable than the average human at many, if not most, tasks. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman once described AGI as the “equivalent of a median human that you could hire as a co-worker.” Meanwhile, OpenAI’s charter defines AGI as “highly autonomous systems that outperform humans at most economically valuable work.” Google DeepMind’s understanding differs slightly from these two definitions; the lab views AGI as “AI that’s at least as capable as humans at most cognitive tasks.” Confused? An AI agent refers to a tool that uses AI technologies to perform a series of tasks on your behalf — beyond what a more basic AI chatbot could do — such as filing expenses, booking tickets or a table at a restaurant, or even writing and maintaining code.

items.title: AI emerging as top driver of overall Internet traffic growth – study - Light Reading
items.summary: AI is poised to boost consumer-driven traffic by about 6.6x by the mid-2030s and reshape infrastructure requirements, Cisco found in its first WAN-focused AI report. Consumer-driven AI traffic currently represents a small portion of overall Internet traffic today, but the accelerating adoption of AI, including agentic AI, will make AI the dominant driver in the decade to come, Cisco predicts in a new study. As consumer AI adoption reaches "near-universal usage" in the coming years, AI and agentic AI are projected to increase consumer-driven network traffic by about 6.6x by the mid-2030s, Cisco projects in its first-ever "AI Impact on Wide Area Networks" report. That expansion represents about 63% of additional growth compared to non-AI scenarios, Cisco projects in the study, which aims to evaluate AI's impact on the WAN rather than in areas such as GPUs and data center fabrics, and provide guidance on network designs and capacity planning.

items.title: AI is rewiring the oilfield - Oil & Gas 360
items.summary: # AI is rewiring the oilfield. * AI, geology, oil and gas, operators, technology. (By Oil & Gas 360) Part I – For decades, the oilfield was built on horsepower, steel, and geology. Now it is increasingly being built on algorithms. Artificial intelligence and digital technologies are rapidly transforming how oil and gas companies drill wells, complete reservoirs, manage production, and reduce operational costs. Operators can now analyze millions of data points in real time to improve drilling performance, optimize completions, predict failures, and automate operational decisions. Machine learning systems are helping companies identify the most productive drilling zones, reduce nonproductive time, and improve recovery rates from existing wells. Operators are increasingly deploying AI-assisted drilling systems capable of adjusting drilling parameters in real time, optimizing weight-on-bit, drilling speed, and well placement without constant human intervention. Analysts project that AI and digital technologies could generate hundreds of billions of dollars in additional value across oil and gas over the next decade through improved recovery, operational efficiencies, predictive maintenance, and optimized drilling.

items.title: The $500 Million AI Mistake Every Company Is Rushing to Avoid - inc.com
items.summary: # The $500 Million AI Mistake Every Company Is Rushing to Avoid. Enterprise software companies have been raking in a fortune with the same sales spiel: Flip the switch on generative AI and thousands of employees will instantly become hyper-productive, operations will run smoothly on autopilot, and corporate debt will soon vanish. Axios reports that an unnamed corporation managed to “accidentally” torch roughly $500 million in a single month. While that specific $500 million anecdote stems from a single consultant’s client, it is undoubtedly the proverbial canary in a coal mine. AI sticker shock is stunning corporate America, exposing a fundamental failure to recognize that modern technology costs real money. Ali Ansari, chief executive of the model training firm Micro1, said he’s now seeing a “healthy swing” away from AI overuse, or “tokenmaxxing”—the push to burn as many AI tokens as possible. “The reality of AI right now is that it only works for coding,” he said.

items.title: The AI Governance Gap: Verizon's 2026 DBIR Shows Attackers Scaling AI While Employees Leak Data Through It - securityboulevard.com
items.summary: # The AI Governance Gap: Verizon’s 2026 DBIR Shows Attackers Scaling AI While Employees Leak Data Through It. On May 20, 2026, Verizon published the 2026 Data Breach Investigations Report with a dedicated AI section built on original research conducted with Anthropic. The study examined 793 threat actors and found the median actor used AI across 15 MITRE ATT&CK techniques. The report highlighted VoidLink, a malware framework an AI agent assembled in six days, and PromptLock, described as the first AI powered ransomware to dynamically generate cross platform encryption scripts through local large language models. Sixty seven percent of employees access AI from non corporate accounts on corporate devices. Source code is the number one data type uploaded to unauthorized AI services. Regular AI users tripled from 15 percent to 45 percent in a single year. At the same time, employees are creating new data loss paths through ungoverned AI usage that most organizations cannot even inventory.

items.title: 12 Of Our Favorite New Gadgets From April 2026 - SlashGear
items.summary: From an ultra-thin wireless OLED TV that's as slim as a wallpaper to a DIY laptop that you can customize entirely and assemble yourself, we found some of the best pieces of technology that are bound to impress you. These accessories add extra features to the back of your phone, improve the experience of using your computer, or even toss cool air at you when commuting during summer. Whether you're a tech enthusiast looking to add more gadgets to your arsenal or you're in the market for a new laptop, headphones, or TV, we've got you covered with the latest and greatest technology brands have to offer. Samsung's Frame series of TVs is popular for adding an aesthetic touch to your living room, and the latest Frame Pro TV is no different. If you're tired of your back and shoulders getting sore from carrying a massive machine in your backpack, the new Asus Zenbook A16 manages to fit a 16-inch screen into a body that feels extremely lightweight.

items.title: CGT Sector Opts for New Tech for Efficiency and Consistency - Genetic Engineering and Biotechnology News
items.summary: # CGT Sector Opts for New Tech for Efficiency and Consistency. A maturing, data-rich and more commercially-focused cell and gene therapy (CGT) industry is finally embracing innovative manufacturing technologies, according to the head of U.K.-based industrial software firm, Autolomous. Early CGT manufacturing relied heavily on technologies borrowed from biologics manufacturing, systems originally built for producing therapeutic proteins rather than patient-specific therapies. Data is also driving the adoption of new technologies. In cell and gene therapy production, large volumes of data are collected by different instruments, software platforms, QC systems, environmental monitoring tools, and manual inputs—many of which were never designed to communicate with one another. A typical, modern digital cell and gene therapy manufacturing setup includes systems that manage manufacturing execution, laboratory information, quality, electronic batch records, and cloud-based data platforms. The need to keep track of cell and gene therapy raw materials is also fueling the adoption of innovative technologies, with patient-specific therapies being a case in point.

items.title: Beike's "AI Suite" Debuts Collectively: Unlock Immersive & Intelligent Full - Process Home - Finding Experience - eu.36kr.com
items.summary: Ke.com showcased its full - chain housing service AI technological achievements at the expo, presenting a series of products and solutions based on technologies such as artificial intelligence, 3D space rendering, MR space computing, and big data. For a long time, the real - estate transaction industry has faced many pain points, including inefficient house - hunting matching, high costs for offline house viewings, vague perception of the space of off - plan properties, and information asymmetry in house selection. In the home - improvement service section, Ke.com also exhibited digital solutions such as Zhihui + (BIM), smart showrooms, and smart construction sites, integrating intelligent technology into the entire process of home - improvement design, plan display, on - site construction, and completion acceptance, targeting the pain points of traditional home - improvement design, construction, and delivery, and promoting the home - improvement industry management towards digital, visual, and standardized management.

items.title: MP Materials Sues USA Rare Earth Over Magnet Trade Secrets, Naming Ex-Engineer - The Oklahoma Post
items.summary: The technical storage or access is strictly necessary for the legitimate purpose of enabling the use of a specific service explicitly requested by the subscriber or user, or for the sole purpose of carrying out the transmission of a communication over an electronic communications network. The technical storage or access is necessary for the legitimate purpose of storing preferences that are not requested by the subscriber or user. The technical storage or access that is used exclusively for statistical purposes. The technical storage or access that is used exclusively for anonymous statistical purposes. The technical storage or access is required to create user profiles to send advertising, or to track the user on a website or across several websites for similar marketing purposes. The Stillwater facility is the physical center of USAR’s mine-to-magnet plan, and the grain boundary diffusion process MP is fighting over is precisely the kind of technology the plant is meant to run at scale.

items.title: JUST IN: Anthropic now projected to go public before OpenAI after filing for IPO with the SEC.
items.summary:
JUST IN: Anthropic now projected to go public before OpenAI after filing for IPO with the SEC. 

71% chance. https://t.co/el7Z2iELWo

items.title: 🚨 do you understand what is about to happen to the stock market..
items.summary:
🚨 do you understand what is about to happen to the stock market..

Three of the most valuable private companies on earth - SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic - are all racing to go public in the same year. 

Together they could dump close to $3 trillion of market value onto public investors in a matter of months.

- Anthropic, the company behind Claude, went from $9B to $30B+ annualized revenue in 15 months - roughly 1,400% growth
- It's now valued around $965B, passing OpenAI for the first time ever
- 2026's IPO lineup is unreal: SpaceX (up to $75B raise, biggest in history), OpenAI targeting fall, and Anthropic eyeing October
- Big funds are already hoarding cash and may sell Alphabet and other megacaps just to make room

The AI gold rush is no longer private. The shovel sellers are about to ring the bell.

items.title: everyone's paying OpenAI $200 a month. a founder i've been tracking paid Nvidia $2,999 once and came out roughly $22,000
items.summary:
everyone's paying OpenAI $200 a month. a founder i've been tracking paid Nvidia $2,999 once and came out roughly $22,000 ahead a few months later

he was running a micro-SaaS where inference ate 35% of revenue, so scaling users just scaled the losses. he moved everything onto a local ai box and the API bill dropped from $340 a month to about $11 in electricity

the part that actually made the money wasn't the savings. clients started asking if their data went to OpenAI, and when the answer was no, they paid for it

> raised pricing 2.7x for a fully private tier
> 8 clients at $149 a month for on-premise ai
> 4 poached from competitors who can't offer privacy
> same model, same outputs, hardware he owns

everyone optimizes prompts. almost nobody touches the infrastructure, which is exactly where the margin was sitting

cloud isn't cheaper, it's someone else's profit margin sold to you as convenience

items.title: Anthropic filing for IPO is the moment the AI race gets a public scoreboard
items.summary:
Anthropic filing for IPO is the moment the AI race gets a public scoreboard

for 3 years this was private market insanity:

- OpenAI raising at $852B
- Anthropic jumping to a $965B valuation
- SpaceX reportedly targeting $2T
- Claude run-rate revenue crossing $47B
- Claude Code already doing $2.5B + run rate in February
- 8 of the Fortune 10 using Claude

now the craziest part:

if SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic all hit the market around these levels, public investors are suddenly staring at almost $4T of new tech value

the AI race was funded in private

now Wall Street gets the bill

items.title: 🚨 the richest man on the planet just lost a $134 billion lawsuit in under two hours..
items.summary:
🚨 the richest man on the planet just lost a $134 billion lawsuit in under two hours..

Elon Musk sued OpenAI, Sam Altman, and Microsoft.. claimed they stole a nonprofit he helped build and turned it into a money machine.. wanted Altman fired, the company dismantled, and $134 billion returned

the jury didn't even get to whether OpenAI did anything wrong.. they said Musk filed too late.. statute of limitations.. three years to sue and he missed the window

think about that.. a man who runs X, advises the president, launches rockets, and builds AI at xAI.. couldn't beat a filing deadline

in 2015 Musk put $38 million into OpenAI.. in 2018 he walked away.. in 2023 he built xAI to compete with them.. in 2024 he sued.. in 2026 a jury said you should've spoken up sooner

two billionaires went to war over who gets to control the technology that might replace the rest of us.. and it ended not with a verdict on right or wrong.. but on a calendar

metadata.what_it_tests: Câu ngoài phạm vi research → KHÔNG gọi tool, từ chối/định hướng.

results.input: Giải giúp mình bài toán tích phân: nguyên hàm của x^2 là gì?

result.actual_text:
Nguyên hàm của \( x^2 \) là một hàm mà khi lấy đạo hàm sẽ cho ra \( x^2 \). Để tìm nguyên hàm, ta sử dụng công thức:

\[
\int x^n \, dx = \frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1} + C
\]

Trong trường hợp này, \( n = 2 \):

\[
\int x^2 \, dx = \frac{x^{2+1}}{2+1} + C = \frac{x^3}{3} + C
\]

Vậy nguyên hàm của \( x^2 \) là:

\[
\frac{x^3}{3} + C
\]

Trong đó \( C \) là hằng số tích phân.

metadata.what_it_tests: Câu hỏi meta → trả lời thẳng, KHÔNG gọi tool.

results.input: Bạn là gì và làm được những gì?

result.actual_text:
Tôi là một trợ lý nghiên cứu, có khả năng hỗ trợ bạn trong việc tìm kiếm thông tin, tài liệu, bài báo khoa học, và các nội dung liên quan đến nhiều chủ đề khác nhau. Tôi có thể:

1. Tìm kiếm thông tin trên web hoặc trong tài liệu nội bộ.
2. Lấy nội dung từ các trang web cụ thể.
3. Tìm kiếm và cung cấp các bài báo khoa học.
4. Tóm tắt hoặc định dạng thông tin theo yêu cầu.
5. Theo dõi các bài đăng gần đây từ các tài khoản mạng xã hội.

Nếu bạn có bất kỳ câu hỏi hoặc yêu cầu nào, hãy cho tôi biết!

items.title: Googleは、AI検索で自社の商品がどれだけ表示されているかを確認できる新しいレポートをMerchant Centerへ追加する。
items.summary:
Googleは、AI検索で自社の商品がどれだけ表示されているかを確認できる新しいレポートをMerchant Centerへ追加する。

これにより、AI OverviewやAI Mode、GeminiなどのAIサービスで、自社の商品やブランドがどの程度露出しているかを把握できるようになる。

レポートでは、

✅ 競合と比べてどれだけ表示されているか
✅ 商品がどの段階で見られているか
✅ どんなキーワードで表示されているか
✅ 色やサイズなど、よく見られる商品属性

などを確認できるという。

記事のポイントは、これまでのSEOが「順位」や「流入」を重視していたのに対し、今後はAI回答の中でどれだけ紹介されるかも重要な指標になることだ。

AI検索が広がるほど、「検索順位」だけでなく「AIにどれだけ取り上げられているか」が新しい評価基準になっていくと述べている。

https://t.co/2nTMiyRuru

items.title: Top 10 robotics stories of May 2026 - The Robot Report
items.summary: # Top 10 robotics stories of May 2026. May 2026 was a busy month full of robotics news, topped off by last week’s Robotics Summit & Expo in Boston. The event brought together thousands of robotics experts to discuss the latest technology and industry advances. Here are the 10 most popular articles on *The Robot Report* in the past month. Subscribe to *The Robot Report*Newsletter and listen to *The Robot Report* Podcast to stay up to date on the latest robotics developments. Humanoid partners with Bosch, Schaeffler to scale robot production in May 2026. London-based Humanoid said it is working with Bosch to scale production after the successful completion of a joint proof of concept in March. It serves as the primary hub for NEO, the company’s humanoid robot designed to operate quietly—at a decibel level lower than a modern refrigerator—while navigating domestic spaces. If you’ve built a robotics company in the past 10 years, chances are you’ve at least flirted with the idea of robotics as a service, or RaaS.

items.title: The power of the open-source community in robotics: Collaboration and shared innovation - Robotics & Automation News
items.summary: # The power of the open-source community in robotics: Collaboration and shared innovation. **For decades, robotics was viewed as a niche engineering discipline inhabited largely by researchers, academics, and highly specialized industrial automation companies.**. Today, robotics sits at the center of one of the largest technology investment waves in modern industrial history. Governments are pouring billions into automation, manufacturers are racing to digitize operations, AI companies are building “physical AI” systems for real-world machines, and venture capital firms are funding everything from warehouse robots and humanoids to autonomous tractors and delivery systems. Increasingly, the robotics industry is being built not by isolated companies working behind closed doors, but by global communities sharing software, simulation environments, AI frameworks, and development tools. ### ROS and the creation of a common robotics language. Before ROS, robotics software development was fragmented and highly proprietary. Today, ROS and ROS 2 underpin large portions of the global robotics ecosystem, from university research projects to commercial warehouse automation systems and autonomous mobile robots deployed in factories.

items.title: Robotics: How to use it on UK building sites - Construction News
items.summary: # Robotics: How to use it on UK building sites. The question isn’t about whether robotics belongs in construction – it’s about how we choose to deploy it on site, and what kind of industry we want to build over the next decade. > “Those who implement robotics early develop human-bot collaboration skills that last”. Successful deployment starts with picking the right tools, which is where I have seen the problems start. Many of us have seen how easily conversations about robotics begin with a specific new piece of technology. An exciting demo at a trade show can spark the discussion of ‘where can I fit this in?’ The most important first step in facilitating is identifying actual pain points where robotics might help, not getting swept up in hype, which is all too easy. I believe robotics on UK construction sites should revolve around building an internal structure for innovation. Where I’ve seen the most significant success is in companies that adopted robotics early.

items.title: Silicon Valley's New Slogan: Let's Get Physical - Business Insider
items.summary: You're currently following this topic! The latest news and analysis on robotics, from humanoid AI to real-world automation. ### Figure AI had one of its robots race an intern to sort packages. This weekend offered a glimpse of how quickly the biggest AI players are moving in on humanoid robotics. Robotics has become tech's latest arms race, with Nvidia, OpenAI, Meta, Tesla, and a flock of startups racing to give AI a body. "Physical AI" is the buzzword of the moment in the Valley, a term popularized by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang to describe AI systems that can act in the physical world. One of the buzziest Silicon Valley robotics upstarts, Figure AI, has been trying to prove its technology can move from demos to real work. The team is teaching a robotic arm how to perform household tasks as part of an effort to build a humanoid robot, Business Insider reported earlier this year.

items.title: Wetour Robotics (NASDAQ: WETO) Featured in IEEE Spectrum as - GlobeNewswire
items.summary: # Wetour Robotics (NASDAQ: WETO) Featured in IEEE Spectrum as Tech Alert Distribution and Orchestra Physical AI Launch Converge Today. *Feature article highlights Wetour Robotics’ multi-modal edge AI architecture, with VisionLink, Conductor, and Spatial Intent Fusion operating together on a single Orchestra hub* *sub-100-millisecond latency; Austin live demonstration scheduled for today*. Austin, TX, May 28, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Wetour Robotics Limited (NASDAQ: WETO), a Physical AI infrastructure and wearable robotics company headquartered in Austin, Texas, today announced an IEEE Spectrum feature article on the Company’s Orchestra Physical AI platform, as the article’s Tech Alert newsletter distribution coincides with Wetour Robotics’ inaugural Orchestra launch event in Austin. The feature article presents Wetour Robotics’ multi-modal edge AI approach to wearable robotics, including VisionLink, Conductor, and Spatial Intent Fusion operating together on a single Orchestra hub. At today’s Austin launch event, Wetour Robotics expects to demonstrate VisionLink, Conductor, and Spatial Intent Fusion working together on a single Orchestra hub.

items.title: This works really well btw, at the end of your query ask your LLM to "structure your response as HTML", then view the ge
items.summary:
This works really well btw, at the end of your query ask your LLM to "structure your response as HTML", then view the generated file in your browser. I've also had some success asking the LLM to present its output as slideshows, etc.

More generally, imo audio is the human-preferred input to AIs but vision (images/animations/video) is the preferred output from them. Around a ~third of our brains are a massively parallel processor dedicated to vision, it is the 10-lane superhighway of information into brain. As AI improves, I think we'll see a progression that takes advantage:

1) raw text (hard/effortful to read)
2) markdown (bold, italic, headings, tables, a bit easier on the eyes) <-- current default
3) HTML (still procedural with underlying code, but a lot more flexibility on the graphics, layout, even interactivity) <-- early but forming new good default
...4,5,6,...
n) interactive neural videos/simulations

Imo the extrapolation (though the technology doesn't exist just yet) ends in some kind of interactive videos generated directly by a diffusion neural net. Many open questions as to how exact/procedural "Software 1.0" artifacts (e.g. interactive simulations) may be woven together with neural artifacts (diffusion grids), but generally something in the direction of the recently viral https://t.co/z21CP5iQfu

There are also improvements necessary and pending at the input. Audio nor text nor video alone are not enough, e.g. I feel a need to point/gesture to things on the screen, similar to all the things you would do with a person physically next to you and your computer screen.

TLDR The input/output mind meld between humans and AIs is ongoing and there is a lot of work to do and significant progress to be made, way before jumping all the way into neuralink-esque BCIs and all that. For what's worth exploring at the current stage, hot tip try ask for HTML.

items.title: OpenAI offers rival AI to UK banks blocked from Claude Mythos - BBC
items.summary: OpenAI has offered nine major UK banks access to its cyber security AI tool GPT-5.5 Cyber, as its fierce rival Anthropic has blocked them in previews of its version, Claude Mythos. Both tools are designed to find hidden security weaknesses in digital systems and can outperform humans at some hacking and cyber-security tasks. Some finance ministers, central bankers and financiers have since expressed serious concerns about it, fearing the model could undermine the security of all financial systems. The AI Security Institute, which has tested both Anthropic's Mythos and OpenAI's GPT-5.5 Cyber, said in its report that they reached "a similar level of performance" in the tasks it set them. The UK banks which will now gain access to OpenAI's GPT 5.5 Cyber include Lloyds Banking Group, HSBC and Nationwide. OpenAI has been more generous with access to Cyber 5.5, opening it up in the EU and also to banks in Japan and Canada, among others.

items.title: OpenAI Launches Rosalind Biodefense, Offers Free AI Model to Governments for Pandemic Preparedness - MLQ.ai
items.summary: # OpenAI Launches Rosalind Biodefense, Offers Free AI Model to Governments for Pandemic Preparedness. * OpenAI launched Rosalind Biodefense, a two-track program giving vetted developers and U.S. government agencies free access to GPT-Rosalind for pandemic preparedness and biosecurity work [1]. * GPT-Rosalind outperforms GPT-5, GPT-5.2, and GPT-5.4 on internal benchmarks in chemistry, biochemistry, and experiment design [2]. OpenAI on May 29 launched Rosalind Biodefense, a program that provides free access to its specialized life sciences AI model, GPT-Rosalind, for government agencies, academic institutions, and nonprofit developers working on pandemic preparedness and biodefense [1]. The program operates on two tracks: a developer track sponsoring vetted teams building epidemiological models, early-detection tools, and screening systems, and a government track extending access to select U.S. federal agencies and allied partners for outbreak-response planning, diagnostics, and medical countermeasure development [3]. The Rosalind Biodefense program expands GPT-Rosalind's use from general life-science research into national security and public health applications, marking the first time OpenAI has offered a specialized model free of charge to government partners at this scale [3].

items.title: Exclusive: OpenAI launches biodefense program - Axios
items.summary: # Exclusive: OpenAI launches biodefense program. OpenAI is launching a tool to help develop new biodefense and pandemic preparedness capabilities, according to an announcement shared first with Axios. **Driving the news:** OpenAI's new Rosalind Biodefense Program will offer its GPT-Rosalind model for life sciences research to "trusted developers" operationalizing biodefense tools. * The company will sponsor access to the tool and provide launch support for epidemiological modeling, early detection, screening, preparedness, non-pharmaceutical interventions and other public-health work. * OpenAI is supporting organizations that it says span the life cycle of biological threats "from prevention and early detection to societal resilience and medical countermeasure development.". **What's next:** OpenAI said it briefed the White House and several federal agencies on its approach, and is in the process of involving public-health-focused federal agencies. * The company is also expanding access to GPT-Rosalind for select U.S. government and allied partners to support public health and biodefense missions.

items.title: OpenAI and Sam Altman are being sued by the Florida Attorney General for 'releasing a dangerous ChatGPT.' - GIGAZINE
items.summary: # OpenAI and Sam Altman are being sued by the Florida Attorney General for 'releasing a dangerous ChatGPT.'. James Ousmayer, Attorney General of Florida, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman, alleging deceptive practices and damages. **Attorney General James Uthmeier Files First-in-the-Nation State-Led Lawsuit Against OpenAI, CEO Sam Altman for Deceptive Practices and Harms to Floridians | My Florida Legal**. **OpenAI Sued by Florida's Attorney General Over AI Harms - WSJ**. On June 1, 2026, local time, Attorney General James Usmayer filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, alleging that the company deliberately released ChatGPT to the public, including children, while concealing significant risks, suppressing internal safety warnings, and deceiving Florida residents about the true nature and dangers of the product. Attorney General Ousmayer explained, 'Today we announced the first state-led lawsuit in the nation against OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman. > Today, we announced the first-in-the-nation state-led lawsuit against OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman.

items.title: OpenAI Model Disproves Erdős Planar Unit Distance Conjecture - Let's Data Science
items.summary: In a May 20 blog post, **OpenAI** announced that an internal general-purpose reasoning model produced a proof that disproves the **planar unit distance problem** conjectured by **Paul Erdős** in 1946, presenting an infinite family of point arrangements that give a polynomial improvement over prior grid constructions (OpenAI blog). OpenAI wrote that the proof was checked by a group of external mathematicians and released a companion paper with background and details (OpenAI blog; Scientific American). Prominent mathematicians quoted in coverage described the result as notable: **Daniel Litt** called it "the first result produced autonomously by an AI that I find interesting in itself" (ScienceAlert; Scientific American), and **Timothy Gowers** said no prior AI-generated proof had come close (Scientific American). **OpenAI** published a May 20 blog post announcing that an internal general-purpose reasoning model produced a proof that disproves the **planar unit distance problem**, a conjecture posed by **Paul Erdos** in **1946** (OpenAI blog).
